methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $96M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Type One Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Antares is a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Jordan Bramble and Julia DeWahl, having raised $96M+ to date. The nuclear energy sector comp universe includes 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Type One Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo. The narrow peer set for Series B stage nuclear companies proved insufficient—only 1 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage comparable identified—preventing reliable peer-median valuation.

A stage-progression methodology was applied, treating total capital raised ($96M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage step-ups in the sector. This approach is appropriate given the early-stage, capital-intensive nature of advanced nuclear development and yielded an implied valuation of $182M. Conservative and upside scenarios, bracketing execution and market timing risk, establish a range from $155M (low) to $210M (high).

The analysis supports a valuation of $182M median, with a plausible range of $155M–$210M. The primary limitation is reliance on a single method; peer multiples could not be reliably applied due to insufficient narrow-set comparables. Key uncertainties include technology de-risking trajectory, regulatory pathway clarity, and nearterm funding environment for the nuclear sector.
